13.reason: This line does not skip whitespace, causing spaces to be stored as operators, leading to incorrect parsing.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  This line does not skip whitespace, causing spaces to be stored as operators, leading to incorrect parsing.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  This line reads any character including whitespace, but the expression may contain spaces and the operator should be read after skipping spaces. As a result, fu[i] may contain spaces, which are not valid operators and cause further logic to misinterpret the expression.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The %c format specifier does not skip whitespace, causing spaces in the input to be incorrectly read as operators and breaking the parsing logic. It should be " %c" to skip leading whitespace.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  scanf("%c") does not skip whitespace characters, so spaces in the expression are incorrectly read as operators, causing misalignment between the operator and number arrays. It should be " %c" to automatically skip leading whitespace.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Using %c reads every character including whitespace; this will break parsing if there are spaces between tokens. Also, i starts at 1 but t and ts indexing assumes consistent alignment.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Reading single char after %d leaves newline or space in buffer; also, this reads one operator per loop but doesn't skip whitespace, causing misalignment between operators and numbers]

11.reason: [This assumes the expression starts with a number, but there may be leading spaces before the first number, causing scanf to fail or behave unexpectedly.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [This assumes the expression starts with a number, but input may start with spaces or even a negative number (though problem says valid expression without parentheses; however, leading space causes scanf to fail reading operator correctly later)]

19.reason: [If the previous scanf("%c") read a space or newline, this may fail to parse the next number correctly, especially if input has irregular spacing.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [If the previous scanf("%c") read a space or newline, this may fail to parse the next number correctly, especially if input has irregular spacing.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [If previous operator read was actually a space or newline due to unhandled whitespace, this will fail to read the next number correctly]

31.reason: [Index j may be out of sync with i; j is only incremented for '+'/'-', so for sequences like "2*3*4", j remains 0 and t[-1] could be accessed if j=0 and j-1=-1 (but here j starts at 1, so t[0] is used — however, logic is fragile and relies on correct initialization).]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Index j may be out of sync with i; j is only incremented for '+'/'-', so for sequences like "2*3*4", j remains 0 and t[-1] could be accessed if j=0 and j-1=-1 (but here j starts at 1, so t[0] is used — however, logic is fragile and relies on correct initialization).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Index j may be out of sync because j is only incremented for '+'/'-', but i increments every time; leads to incorrect array access and logic]
